steps.title: Identify the key signal — what “moderate” means
steps.content: The passage concludes that store context may “moderate the effect.” In research, “moderate” means to reduce or weaken. The original effect: products spaced close together are perceived as significantly more valuable than those spaced far apart. The prediction: this spacing effect should be weaker at an inexpensive store (Forever 21) than at an upscale store (Tiffany & Co.).

steps.title: Find the choice showing the spacing effect weakened at the inexpensive store
steps.content:
Choice C shows that at the upscale store (Tiffany & Co.) the spacing effect appeared (far apart judged substantially more valuable than close together), but at the inexpensive store (Forever 21) the same effect was only slight. That weakening of the spacing pattern at the inexpensive store directly supports the researchers' conclusion. The other choices either describe a uniform pattern across both stores or reverse the relationship.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
